title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Runtime Trace: Async Testing
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js async testing increment in a tested and release-ready JavaScript application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.
Use fakes, stubs, spies, and controlled clocks only at owned boundaries without mocking away the behavior under test.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Async Testing
steps.instruction:
theory case — A release candidate passes the familiar 5-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Async Testing contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Trace concrete runtime values, bindings, receiver, mutation, and queued work in order; assert the observable result, then change one input or event and seek the first causal divergence. Apply that move specifically to Async Testing while producing a tested and release-ready JavaScript application.
